mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during documented rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, consumer spending power collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales rise alongside CPIAUCSL increases above 0.5% monthly, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance with Rank IC below -0.02 when applied outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect growing consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months following housing starts increases, or the correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day returns falls below 0.05 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly consumer price index increases exceeding 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of instances following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4%, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance at p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of cases when payrolls rise alongside falling unemployment, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample regression tests with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion, which supports higher corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth exceeds 2.0% but is accompanied by rising interest rates, or predictive accuracy drops below 50% during late-cycle phases in walk-forward testing.
